owner.notesMd: The collection demonstrates UX corpus shape, not runtime implementation authority.

governingArtifacts.notesMd: Defines the owning Jobs search and filters behavior surface.
Desired requirements are implementation targets when confirmed governing.
Mappings distinguish preserved, changed, added, removed, and pending behavior.

rationale.summary: Jobs filters is behavior-critical enough to require explicit hierarchy, requirements, mappings, pending decisions, and proof rows.
rationale.whyThisProfile: The example models existing behavior preservation plus desired UX decisions for refactor-safe implementation.
rationale.whyThisDepth: Acceptance criteria are included so code can be judged against UX behavior rather than runtime habit.
rationale.riskNotesMd: The main risks are silently changing clear/apply semantics or treating observed current behavior as desired behavior.

allowedOmissions.reason: Pixel-level layout is not required for this refactor-preservation example.
allowedOmissions.revisitWhen: Responsive ordering, modal hierarchy, or progressive disclosure becomes part of the behavior contract.

allowedOmissions.reason: Only behavior-relevant accessibility should be included until a focused minute-detail pass is requested.
allowedOmissions.revisitWhen: Focus, keyboard, screen-reader, or reduced-motion behavior changes.

blockedAreas.reason: Exact copy is behaviorally meaningful and remains pending confirmation.
blockedAreas.unblocksWhen: The user confirms whether exact Clear Filters copy should become governing.

pendingDecisions.title: Confirm exact Clear Filters confirmation copy
